title: [lichen_l2.c:863-873] NET_DEVICE_INIT priority could race with other init
description:
Gap found: Device initialization order not reviewed.

Why this was missed: Zephyr initialization framework interaction not examined.

The actual bug: NET_DEVICE_INIT at lines 863-873 uses CONFIG_KERNEL_INIT_PRIORITY_DEFAULT for initialization priority. This determines when lichen_l2_iface_init() runs relative to other initializations.

Potential issues:
1. If LoRa driver (lora0) initializes at a later priority, lichen_lora_l2_init() could fail
2. If hwinfo driver initializes later, generate_eui64() could fail
3. If tinycrypt initializes later, lichen_sha256() could fail

Zephyr typically initializes drivers before application code, and CONFIG_KERNEL_INIT_PRIORITY_DEFAULT is correct for network interfaces. The code handles failures gracefully (sets iface_init_failed).

Verified correct ordering for standard Zephyr configurations. Priority 4 - platform-specific concern for custom boards.
close_reason: Fixed: Added documentation comment to NET_DEVICE_INIT explaining why CONFIG_KERNEL_INIT_PRIORITY_DEFAULT is correct for network interfaces, noting that Zephyr initializes hardware drivers at earlier priorities, and that the init function gracefully handles missing dependencies via iface_init_failed.
